meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop chasing tutorials and start building. Use this intermediate checklist to implement analytics in your AI apps using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you measure real-world usage. By integrating lightweight analytics, you transform your project from a static code snippet into a data-driven product. This checklist provides the exact workflow to instrument your app, track user behavior, and prove your engineering competence to future employers.
content.intro:
You’ve watched the videos, followed the repo, and deployed the boilerplate. Yet, the moment you step away from the tutorial, the screen turns into an abyss. You aren't learning to build; you're learning to mimic. The difference between a tutorial-follower and a software engineer is the ability to observe how real users interact with your code.

True mastery begins with instrumentation. By embedding analytics into your first AI project, you move from passive consumption to active observation. This isn't just about tracking clicks—it's about building a feedback loop that validates your architecture and proves your worth as a developer.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Don't track everything. Identify the one action that proves your user found value in your AI tool.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and suggest 3 high-signal user events to track that define 'success' for a first-time AI app user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify key user interaction events in src/app.tsx'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Setup a lightweight tracking utility that doesn't bloat your bundle size or compromise performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular analytics wrapper that handles event batching and prevents blocking the main thread.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'create lib/analytics.ts with a clean interface for tracking events'

steps.title: Instrument Key AI Model Latency
steps.description: Measure how long your AI requests take. This is a critical metric for any AI-native application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a higher-order function to wrap my API calls that automatically records the duration of AI model inference.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'wrap api/chat.ts calls with performance monitoring logs'

steps.title: Automate Error Tracking
steps.description: Catch bugs before your users do. Silent failures are the primary cause of abandonment in student projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a global error boundary that logs component crashes and API failures to my analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement error boundary in components/ErrorBoundary.tsx'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate Data Flow
steps.description: Verify that your events are hitting the server correctly without polluting your development environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script to simulate user behavior and verify that analytics events are firing as expected.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create test/analytics.test.ts to verify event payload structure'

pro_tips: Track 'time-to-first-token' for your AI responses; it is the most important metric for user retention in LLM apps.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to your analytics provider; hash user IDs instead.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to keep your analytics costs low during the prototype phase.
Tag your events with the model version (e.g., gpt-4o vs claude-3-5) to compare performance via A/B testing.
Set up a 'dead-man's switch' alert if your event volume drops to zero, indicating your tracking script broke.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, share your dashboard screenshots on LinkedIn to prove your project's impact, then start adding a feature based on the data you've gathered.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the roadmap to stop being a student and start being a builder. Don't let your code sit in the dark. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
